text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful method is to set SMART goals—Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," a SMART goal would be "increase website traffic by 20% within three months." Once goals are defined, break them into milestones, such as completing the design phase or launching a new feature. Each milestone should have specific deliverables, like a report or a prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved, including team members and clients. This way, all parties agree on the direction and expectations, which helps avoid confusion later.
